name: Date Range Picker
summary: A named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a from–to date range. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a calendar grid) and granularity=range (two coordinated, ordered dates), bound to two native <input type="date"> anchors (start, end). The range coordination — the start date can't cross the end date, reported as a pair — is the core's machinery, exactly as the slider's dual-thumb range is two bound, ordered values over one track; this preset only pins granularity=range and binds the two anchors.
designDecisions: A preset is a dimension pin, not new machinery (#713 option C) — date-range-picker pins presentation=media + granularity=range over two input[type=date] anchors. The range coordination lives in the `temporal` core (one place), the direct analogue of the slider's dual-thumb range being two coordinated, ordered values over one track — not a re-implementation per picker.

Two native date inputs are the anchor and fallback; the shared calendar grid is the media enhancement, while the pair of native date inputs stays the keyboard-entry, form-associated, no-JS baseline, kept ordered (start ≤ end) by the core.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A from–to range preset over the calendar grid: two coupled date values with range-selection keyboard handling.
